mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate indicates the central bank has limited ammunition for further easing, which increases downside tail risk and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% over any 2-year window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below a 4-month moving average threshold of 5.0% signals labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 5.0% on a monthly basis
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in the 6 months following trigger events are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or average return < 0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a demand-supply imbalance that typically resolves with increased corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not produce average positive 20-day returns > 1% over 10-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% forces the central bank to tighten policy, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. Below that threshold, rising inflation is benign and correlates with positive returns.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are on average positive, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of economic activity; when they are increasing, it signals future growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns are not positively correlated (spearman >0.1) with HOUST month-over-month changes over a 5-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 0.5% signals the onset of an economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If average 20-day forward return following such declines is positive or not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) accelerates while retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates, it signals that labor income is not translating into consumption, suggesting a potential economic slowdown that is negative for equities.
failure_condition: If the joint signal yields average positive 60-day returns > 2% over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, further rate cuts signal a desperate central bank and are interpreted negatively by markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days. When rates are above 1%, rate cuts are expansionary and positive for stocks.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% the 5-day forward return following a rate cut (negative change in FEDFUNDS) is on average positive, reject.
